title: Workspace Report
description: Combined workspace report payload for installed modules, config posture, diagnostics, and next-action routing.

kind.description: Discriminator identifying the payload or record shape.

canonical_doc.description: Canonical doc text value used by this contract.

command.description: Command string or command name used by this contract.

items.description: One entry in the shared fields list.

shared_fields.description: Fields shared across related report profiles.

items.description: One entry in the report principles list.

report_principles.description: Ordered report principles entries used by this contract.

schema.description: Report schema metadata and the shared fields exposed by this report profile.

command.description: Command invocation represented by this report.

target.description: Resolved repository path the report describes.

invoked_cli_identity.description: Invoked cli identity referenced contract shape used by this contract.

cli_compatibility.description: Cli compatibility referenced contract shape used by this contract.

items.description: One entry in the selected modules list.

selected_modules.description: Modules selected for this report invocation.

items.description: One entry in the installed modules list.

installed_modules.description: Installed Agentic Workspace modules detected in the target repository.

feature_tier.description: Resolved feature tier, package footprint, and ordinary operating scope.

health.description: Overall workspace health signal for quick routing.

report_profile.description: Report profile metadata, including which sections are decision-grade.

output_contract.description: Output contract details used by this contract.

operating_posture.description: Current operating posture for agent work in this repository.

task_posture_packet.description: Optional report-visible dynamic instruction packet with selected posture, module contributions, and provenance.

maintainer_mode.description: Package-maintainer dogfooding report routes enabled by workspace.maintainer_mode.

config_enforcement.description: How repo config expectations are applied and reported.

config_effect_audit.description: Audit of each config setting's concrete force, affected output routes, and agent dependency.

branch_workflow_posture.description: Branch and worktree posture relevant to safe continuation.

local_memory.description: Machine-local memory configuration and advisory state.

memory_consult.description: Recommended checked-in Memory lookup route for the current report.

routine_work_context.description: Assembled routine router projection grouping existing owner surfaces into authority, active work, evidence/proof, durable knowledge, and promotion/residue; configured or learned Memory matches remain owner-surface evidence, not AW-owned semantic classification.

reuse_pressure.description: Entry point for changed-path reuse and abstraction-pressure facts.

agent_aids.description: Reusable agent aid availability, manifest coverage, and startup role information.

agent_configuration_system.description: How repo-owned agent configuration classes are represented in this workspace.

agent_configuration_queries.description: Compact queries agents should ask before opening broader configuration docs.

system_intent_mirror.description: Compiled system-intent mirror status and source metadata.

durable_intent.description: Compact decision projection for durable task, subsystem, and system intent pressure.

applicable_intent.description: Compact applicable-intent source, authority, freshness, conflict, durable-outcome, and manual-verification evidence.

workflow_obligations.description: Repo-configured workflow obligations surfaced for report consumers.

assurance_requirements.description: Repo-declared assurance requirements, active matches, and evidence status projection; task-marker matches cite explicit config while semantic acceptance remains agent/human owned.

verification.description: Repo-native verification protocols, scenarios, bounded evidence bundles, and soft verification routing projection; task-marker matches are configured protocol evidence, not AW-owned intent classification.

authority_hierarchy.description: Authority and promotion-path guidance for deciding which repo surfaces are current instruction, durable memory, future work, or historical audit.

continuation_state.description: Compact continuation-state contract for preserving only unfinished or handoff-relevant work, not historical residue.

compliance_economics.description: Boundary statement for what Agentic Workspace can enforce directly and where it instead makes noncompliance visible or costly.

final_report_budget.description: Guidance for compact closeout reporting that preserves evidence without restating obvious diffs or raw logs.

product_managed_enclave.description: Product-managed workspace enclave boundaries and ownership signals.

ownership_diagnostics.description: Diagnostics for drift between authority ledgers, startup adapters, config policy, and workflow surfaces.

surface_value_guardrail.description: Durable-surface review guardrail for avoiding unnecessary new surfaces.

effective_authority.description: Resolved authority hierarchy for workspace, module, and repo-owned surfaces.

operational_compression.description: Compression signals that keep ordinary agent startup and reporting cheap.

successful_completion_cost.description: Recent model CLI evaluation cost, package-read overhead, and rework signals for advisory optimization decisions.

completion_contract.description: Derived Planning completion-contract lens for done, partial, blocked, and continuation-required decisions.

parent_intent_status.description: Derived parent/original-intent status packet used to prevent slice proof from being reported as parent completion.

applicable_intent_status.description: Derived applicable-intent evidence and conflict packet used to keep user, system, subsystem, and soft-intent obligations visible before broad closeout.

repair_loop_residue.description: Derived validation-driven repair residue across Planning and Verification.

structured_findings.description: Structured finding residue shape for review and promotion routing.

external_evidence_safety.description: External evidence freshness, divergence, stale-after, and closeout-safety projection.

workflow_compliance_summary.description: Derived review and recovery summary of workflow entrypoint, satisfied or missing gates, trust impact, and recovery action.

authority_boundary.description: Authority boundary showing which closeout/report signals are AW-enforced, observed, recommended, or agent-owned.

intent_evidence.description: Closeout intent provenance showing whether interpreted intent came from planning evidence, user text, issue/reference evidence, handoff evidence, or agent inference.

interpreted_intent.description: Requested outcome, intent-source evidence, satisfaction status, and closure decision rendered for the closeout report.

decision_review.description: Derived review packet for agent-authored system decision facts, owner-model split, completeness, absence routing, and durable-owner guidance.

review_compression.description: Derived closeout-first human review guide naming the selected work-shape mode, first-inspection facts and contract, rendered fact requirements, behavior-preserving refactor semantic-risk focus, detail routes, and human-owned decisions.

validation.description: Closeout validation projection, including proof confidence and behavior_preservation facts when preservation claims, proof classes, unsupported claims, or caveats were recorded.

closeout_adoption.description: Derived closeout quality rubric, representative examples, and current rendering adoption status for human-useful final reports.

completion_gate.description: Closeout transition gate that blocks full completion or closure-language claims when active human intent is unsatisfied and no explicit human-accepted partial scope exists.

closeout_report.description: Derived operator-facing closeout report profile, traceability, completeness, decision-review facts, behavior-preservation proof/caveat facts, closeout-first review-compression contract, closeout adoption rubric, validation, gaps, closure boundary, final-response rendering guidance, and profile-bound rendered closeout summary.

continuation_next_actions.description: Evidence-ranked next actions for safe continuation.

migration_pilot_template.description: Optional migration-pilot decomposition template with parity and rollout boundaries.

compact_output_criteria.description: Criteria for compact CLI outputs to remain sufficient for cheap continuation.

automation_readiness.description: Provider-agnostic automation-readiness checklist that keeps execution outside AW.

decision_pressure.description: Routing-only architecture-decision pressure surface with owner-model split, host decision target discovery, existing decision index, promotion pressure, and command-owned scaffold routes.

maintenance_pressure.description: Maintainer-facing pressure signals and optional follow-up routes.

closeout_trust.description: Trust signals for whether recent or current work can be safely closed.

external_work_reconciliation.description: Provider-agnostic reconciliation between checked-in planning and external work evidence.

external_work_delta.description: Delta view for external work evidence when a prior snapshot is available.

findings.description: Diagnostic findings emitted by the report.

next_action.description: Recommended next action derived from report health and routing signals.

discovery.description: Discovery hints for relevant repo surfaces and compact commands.

standing_intent.description: Standing intent details used by this contract.

improvement_intake.description: Improvement intake details used by this contract.

repo_friction.description: Repo friction details used by this contract.

registry.description: Ordered registry entries used by this contract.

config.description: Config details used by this contract.

reports.description: Ordered reports entries used by this contract.

module_reports.description: Module-owned report fragments included in the combined workspace report.

execution_shape.description: How the current task or report context should be shaped for safe execution.

operating_posture.description: Resolved optimization, artifact, initiative, assurance, and delegation posture for this task.

workflow_obligations.description: Matched workflow obligations with stage, force, scope, and provenance.

skill_routes.description: Task-selected skills, prompts, or routing fragments.

allowed_actions.description: Actions allowed under the resolved posture.

forbidden_actions.description: Actions forbidden under the resolved posture.

proof_boundaries.description: Proof boundaries selected for the current task.

closeout_boundaries.description: Closeout and claim boundaries selected for the current task.

read_budget.description: Read-budget profile and selector/deep-read limits for this task.

authority_boundaries.description: Authority boundaries with source and owner provenance.

output_shape_requirements.description: Output-shape constraints selected for this task.

review_rubrics.description: Review rubrics selected for this task.

module_contributions.description: Matched module posture contributions with trigger and projection provenance.

provenance.description: Config, obligation, module, or command sources used to assemble this packet.

task_posture_packet.description: Task-specific posture and dynamic instruction packet selected from config, task facts, workflow obligations, and module declarations.

kind.description: Discriminator for the parent/original-intent status packet.

status.description: Whether the recorded parent/original intent is satisfied, open, or only guidance.

parent_intent_status.description: Parent/original-intent status preserving larger intent across bounded slices.

kind.description: Discriminator for the applicable-intent status packet.

status.description: Whether applicable-intent evidence is present, guidance-only, or requires attention.

applicable_intent_status.description: Applicable-intent evidence, conflicts, missing authority, and manual-verification obligations.

kind.description: Discriminator for the applicable-intent source projection.

status.description: Whether source evidence is present, guidance-only, or requires attention.

source_count.description: Number of applicable-intent source records surfaced in this projection.

sources.description: Structural or configured source records from Planning, Memory, assurance, Verification, standing intent, durable intent, or external work evidence.

authority_vocabulary.description: Stable vocabulary for authority classes, freshness/trust states, conflict states, and manual-verification states.

conflict_status.description: Compact status for whether the projection has no conflict, a possible conflict, a closeout-blocking conflict, or a clarification requirement.

conflicts.description: Recorded conflict statements from existing owner surfaces.

missing_authority.description: Recorded missing authority or acceptance evidence.

stale_or_superseded.description: Sources whose freshness state indicates stale or superseded evidence.

manual_verification.description: Manual/domain/compliance verification obligations that must remain visible before broad closeout.

durable_outcomes.description: Recorded clarification, accepted-interpretation, waiver, or deferral outcomes with owner, evidence anchor, stale condition, reopen trigger, affected claims, and resolved items.

resolved_outcome_count.description: Number of durable applicable-intent outcome records surfaced by this projection.

durable_outcome_routing.description: Owner-surface guidance for clarifications, accepted interpretations, waivers, and deferred outcomes.

blocked_claims.description: Completion claims that should not be made while applicable-intent conflicts or missing verification remain.

closeout_blocked.description: Whether applicable-intent evidence blocks broad/plain closeout claims.

next_actions.description: Suggested next inspection or resolution actions.

authority_boundary.description: Authority boundary showing that AW assembles structural/configured evidence while agent and human owners decide semantic applicability and acceptance.

rule.description: Usage rule for when this projection should be consulted.

applicable_intent.description: Applicable-intent source projection across existing AW surfaces without semantic conflict resolution.

kind.description: Discriminator for the closeout completion gate.

status.description: State-machine result for the requested completion claim.

active_intent_satisfied.description: Agent-recorded answer to whether delivered work satisfies the active human intent.

human_accepted_partial.description: Whether the human explicitly accepted a partial or narrower completion claim.

claim_level_allowed.description: Highest claim level supported by the gate.

required_next_action.description: Required transition before the workflow can stop or claim completion.

claim_authorization.description: Structured claim classes and closure actions authorized or blocked by the gate.

residual_intent.description: Remaining active human intent when the requested full claim is unsupported.

question.description: Self-review question the agent must answer before making a completion claim.

answer.description: Agent-owned self-review answer for active human intent satisfaction.

reason.description: Reason the self-review answer supports or blocks the requested completion claim.

self_review.description: Mandatory agent self-review before final completion claims.

owner_surface.description: Planning, lane, decomposition, or other durable surface that owns the residual intent.

created_or_required.description: Whether follow-on work has been created or is required before full completion can be claimed.

reason.description: Why continuation or follow-on routing is required.

continuation.description: Durable follow-on route or required continuation state.

authority_boundary.description: Boundary stating AW enforces the transition rule while the agent owns semantic satisfaction judgment and the human owns accepted narrowing.

rule.description: Invariant enforced by the completion gate.

completion_gate.description: Closeout transition gate derived from existing intent, proof, Planning, and closeout fields.

kind.description: Discriminator for structured completion claim authorization.

blocked_claim_classes.description: Claim classes renderers must not use.

closure_actions.description: Structured closure actions such as issue closure for concrete targets.

renderer_rule.description: Renderer rule requiring templates to be selected only from authorized claim classes/actions.

unsafe_claim_examples.description: Human-readable examples of unsafe completion prose for explanation only.

diagnostic_only.description: Marker that unsafe_claim_examples are diagnostics, not enforcement rules.

diagnostics.description: Human-readable examples for explanation only; not the enforcement model.

claim_authorization.description: Structured authorization for completion claim classes and closure actions.

surface.description: Payload surface whose authority categories are being described.

authority_class.description: Dominant authority class for the surface.

enforced_by_aw.description: Hard gates or constraints AW enforces for this surface.

observed_by_aw.description: Mechanical facts, evidence, path state, or configuration AW observed.

recommended_by_aw.description: Advisory next actions or support signals AW recommends without owning semantic judgment.

candidate_routes.description: Possible routes AW surfaced for the agent to evaluate.

proof_hints.description: Proof burden or validation hints relevant to the surface.

agent_owned_decisions.description: Semantic, route, proof-proportionality, or completion judgments the agent owns.

human_owned_decisions.description: Intent, acceptance, or handoff decisions requiring human ownership when present.

reporting_rule.description: How agents should report the boundary without overstating AW authority.

authority_boundary.description: Authority boundary showing what AW enforces, observes, recommends, or leaves to agent and human judgment.
